summary: Mutual non-disclosure agreement defining confidential information, obligations of the receiving party, exclusions, the duration of confidentiality obligations, and other standard terms including notice of immunity for whistleblowers.

findings.issue: Confidentiality term is not compliant with playbook minimum duration for business confidential information.
findings.why: Clause allows confidentiality obligations to end earlier than 3 years upon written notice from Disclosing Party, while playbook expects at least 3 years minimum duration for standard business confidential info.
findings.excerpt: duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends...
findings.recommendation: Revise to ensure confidentiality period for standard confidential information is at least 3 years or longer as required for trade secrets, regardless of early release by Disclosing Party.
findings.suggested_redline: Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after termination, and trade secret obligations survive for as long as the information remains a trade secret.

assumptions: Party names and specific dates are blank in the template provided.
Standard NDA template language is used.
No governing law or jurisdiction specified in this excerpt.
Signature blocks anticipate two parties: Disclosing and Receiving.
missing_info_questions: Are there any side letters or amendments not included in this text?
Is this agreement intended to be mutual or one-way for confidentiality and indemnity obligations?
